rawIntent:
[Feature: Build the leave management module — Phase 1: Create Employee model and repository]

Create src/modules/employee/employee.model.ts with TypeScript interfaces for Employee (attributes: id, name, email, managerId, department, status) and CreateEmployeeDto. Create src/modules/employee/employee.repository.ts with IEmployeeRepository interface and EmployeeRepository class implementing it. Include Jest unit tests in tests/unit/modules/employee/employee.repository.test.ts. This phase has no dependencies on other application modules.

Phase architecture notes:
Employee entity provides identity and reporting hierarchy for leave approvals.

Detailed phase architecture (architecture-agent):

successCriteria.description: src/modules/employee/employee.model.ts exists and exports Employee, CreateEmployeeInput, UpdateEmployeeInput
src/modules/employee/employee.repository.ts exists and exports EmployeeRepository class implementing IEmployeeRepository
EmployeeRepository uses the database pool from src/shared/db/connection.ts for all queries
All state-changing operations (create, update, delete) write an audit record to the audit_logs table with entity_type 'employee' and exclude PII fields (name, email) from old_value/new_value
All state-changing operations execute the primary mutation and the audit log insert within a single database transaction to ensure atomicity
tests/unit/modules/employee/employee.repository.test.ts exists and contains Jest tests for EmployeeRepository covering all CRUD methods and audit logging, with tests passing

constraints: Repository pattern: All database access goes through repository interfaces.
Audit records: All state-changing operations write an audit record.
No sensitive data in logs: Exclude PII fields (name, email) from audit log old_value/new_value.
Error handling: All async errors are caught and handled.
All state-changing operations execute the primary mutation and the audit log insert within a single database transaction to ensure atomicity.
outOfScope: Creating LeavePolicy, LeaveBalance, LeaveRequest, or Notification models or repositories
Integration with other modules (policy, balance, leave, notification)
Any modifications to existing modules outside employee
